title: Module 05 — Connections
problem: Starting from the provided Globe_Valve_SS05_Start.wbpz archive, review and clean up the automatically-created bonded contact regions in the globe valve assembly: inspect contact/target scoping and behavior, trim contact faces where there is a physical gap (bolt shanks vs. holes), rename contacts based on their definition, group repetitive bolt-to-nut contacts, use the Contact Tool to find and suppress unused contact regions, and explore the pinball region setting.

_notes.source: Built from ANSYS Mechanical Getting Started, Release 2023 R1 — Module 05 Student Step-by-Step Guide: Connections.
_notes.selector_status: UNVERIFIED — selectors use display names/labels taken from the source slide screenshots, following the same convention as tut1.json. Resolve real UIA automation_id/name via live testing before relying on them.
_notes.highlight_legend: uia = overlay draws a box around a UIA element. ocr_text = overlay finds on-screen text via OCR (used for the Workbench Toolbox / Project Schematic, which are not exposed to UIA — see tut1.json's workbench_uia_finding note). instruction = 3D-viewport action UIA cannot see; shows a reference screenshot instead of a tracked box. none = no highlight (launch).
_notes.verify_legend: This tutorial is exploratory (review/cleanup of an existing model, no single numeric answer), so every step uses verify.type == "manual" for now, matching tut1's own manual_verify_switch_2026-06 decision to prefer a simple confirm button over fragile OCR/UIA detection for a student-facing pilot.
_notes.report_checks_omitted: No report_checks block: this module doesn't produce a report deliverable or a single expected numeric result, so per the template's guidance the tutorial simply has no report-upload step.
_notes.repeat_step: me_14_repeat_other_bolts stands in for repeating the two-face-trim procedure (me_10-me_13) on the other 7 identically-named 'Bonded - Component1\ValveBody To Component20\bolt' contact regions, mirroring how the source slide collapses this into a single 'repeat the last two slides' instruction.

sections.section: Open the starter project in Workbench

steps.title: Open Ansys Workbench
steps.description: Launch Ansys Workbench: Windows Start Menu button → All apps → ANSYS nn.n → Workbench nn.n.

verify.prompt: Is Ansys Workbench open?

hints: Start it from the Windows Start menu or the lab launcher.

steps.title: Open the starter archive
steps.description: File → Open…, browse for archive file Globe_Valve_SS05_Start.wbpz, click Open, then save it to a convenient location.

verify.prompt: Project opened and saved to a convenient location?

hints: Click File to open the menu, then choose Open…

steps.title: Edit the Model cell
steps.description: Right-click the Model cell in the Static Structural system block and choose Edit… to launch Ansys Mechanical.

verify.prompt: Mechanical opened?

hints: The system block is in the Project Schematic; 'Model' is the row with the lightning-bolt icon.

steps.title: Expand the Connections branch
steps.description: In the Outline tree, expand the Connections branch.

verify.prompt: Connections branch expanded?

hints: Click the small expand arrow to the left of 'Connections'.

steps.title: Expand the Contacts branch
steps.description: Expand the Contacts branch underneath Connections.

verify.prompt: Contacts branch expanded, showing the list of contact regions?

hints: The expand arrow is to the left of 'Contacts'.

steps.title: Review the first contact region's Type
steps.description: Select the first contact region in the tree and review its detail Type.

verify.prompt: Details panel shows this contact's Type?

hints: Type is in the Definition section of the Details panel.

steps.title: Review another contact region's Type
steps.description: Select another contact region and review its detail Type.

verify.prompt: Compared Type between two contact regions?

hints: Pick any other row under Contacts.

steps.title: Review the available contact types
steps.description: Click in the Bonded field, then click the down-arrow to review the menu of available contact types.

verify.prompt: Contact-type dropdown reviewed (do not change the value)?

hints: Type is a dropdown field; click it, then the arrow, to see the list.

steps.title: Rename contacts based on their definition
steps.description: Right-click the Contacts branch and choose 'Rename Based on Definition' so each contact region's name reflects its scoping.

verify.prompt: Contact region names updated to reflect their bodies (e.g. 'Bonded - Component1\ValveBody To Component2\flange')?

hints: Right-click directly on the 'Contacts' branch, not an individual contact.

steps.title: Review Tolerance Slider and Tolerance Value
steps.description: Select the Contacts branch and review the details Tolerance Slider and Tolerance Value.

verify.prompt: Tolerance Slider and Tolerance Value reviewed in Details?

hints: These are in the Definition section when the Contacts branch itself is selected.

steps.title: Review graphics display features
steps.description: Select the first contact region and review its graphics display features (Contact Bodies highlighted red, Target Bodies highlighted blue in the Geometry view).

verify.prompt: Red (contact) and blue (target) highlighting observed in the Geometry view?

hints: Contact = red, Target = blue — this color convention holds throughout Mechanical.

steps.title: Select a ValveBody-to-bolt contact region
steps.description: Select the contact region 'Bonded - Component1\ValveBody To Component20\bolt'. There is a physical gap between the bolt shank and the valve-body hole, so its faces need to be trimmed.

verify.prompt: Contact region selected and Details panel showing Scope?

hints: There are 8 contact regions with this exact name; any one works to start.

steps.title: Activate edit mode for the Target faces
steps.description: Click the '2 Faces' field next to detail Target to activate edit mode for the target surfaces — the Apply and Cancel buttons appear.

verify.prompt: Target field in edit mode (Apply/Cancel buttons visible)?

hints: Target is in the Scope section of Details.

steps.title: Keep only the face under the bolt head
steps.description: In the Target Body View, single-click the surface under the bolt head to select it (or Ctrl+click the bolt shank face to deselect it), then click Apply next to detail Target. Target should now read '1 Face'.

verify.prompt: Detail Target now shows '1 Face'?

hints: Use Ctrl+click to deselect the shank face if both got selected together.

steps.title: Activate edit mode for the Contact faces
steps.description: Click the '2 Faces' field next to detail Contact to activate edit mode for the contact surfaces.

verify.prompt: Contact field in edit mode (Apply/Cancel buttons visible)?

hints: Contact is just above Target in the Scope section of Details.

steps.title: Keep only the outer annular surface
steps.description: In the Contact Body View, single-click the outer annular surface of the valve body to select it (or Ctrl+click the cylindrical hole face to deselect it), then click Apply next to detail Contact. Contact should now read '1 Face'.

verify.prompt: Detail Contact now shows '1 Face'?

hints: Use Ctrl+click to deselect the cylindrical bore face if both got selected together.

steps.title: Repeat for the other 7 bolt contact regions
steps.description: Repeat steps me_10-me_13 (trim Target to 1 face, trim Contact to 1 face) for each of the other 7 contact regions named 'Bonded - Component1\ValveBody To Component20\bolt'.

verify.prompt: All 8 ValveBody-to-bolt contact regions now trimmed to 1 Contact face and 1 Target face?

hints: Letters A-H in the Geometry view mark the 8 bolt locations around the flange.

steps.title: Observe the unneeded flange-to-bolt contacts
steps.description: Select one of the 8 contact regions named 'Bonded - Component2\flange To Component20\bolt'. Observe that these are not needed — there is a significant gap between the outer surfaces of the bolt shanks and the inner surfaces of the holes — but do not make any changes at this time.

verify.prompt: Gap between bolt shank and hole observed; confirmed no changes were made here?

hints: This contact region will later show up as one of the 'orange' unused rows in the Contact Tool worksheet.

steps.title: Select the ValveBody-to-flange contact
steps.description: Select the contact region 'Bonded - Component1\ValveBody To Component2\flange'.

verify.prompt: Contact region selected?

hints: This is the main body-to-flange contact, not a bolt contact.

steps.title: Experiment with surface selection
steps.description: Experiment with surface selection in the Contact Body View and the Target Body View for this contact region.

verify.prompt: Explored selecting/deselecting faces in both Body Views?

hints: No Apply needed here — this step is just for familiarization.

steps.title: Select the 8 nut-to-bolt contact regions
steps.description: Ctrl-select the 8 contact regions named 'Bonded - Component21\nut To Component20\bolt' in the Outline tree.

verify.prompt: All 8 nut-to-bolt contact regions highlighted?

hints: Click the first, then Ctrl+click the remaining 7.

steps.title: Group the selected contacts
steps.description: Right-click any one of the selected contact regions and choose Group.

verify.prompt: A new folder appeared under Contacts containing the 8 selected contacts?

hints: Right-click on any one of the highlighted rows, not empty space.

steps.title: Name the new group folder
steps.description: Enter a name for the new group folder.

verify.prompt: Group folder renamed?

hints: The folder name is in edit mode immediately after grouping; just type and press Enter.

sections.section: Use Go To tools to cross-check bodies and contacts

steps.title: Activate the Body filter
steps.description: Activate the Body filter in the Selection toolbar.

verify.prompt: Body filter button now active/highlighted?

hints: The filter buttons are in the Selection toolbar above the Geometry view.

steps.title: Select the flange body
steps.description: Select part Component2\flange in the Geometry view.

verify.prompt: Component2\flange highlighted as the selected body?

hints: With the Body filter active, a single click selects the whole part.

steps.title: Go To Contacts for Selected Bodies
steps.description: In any background area of the Geometry view, right-click → Go To → Contacts for Selected Bodies. Observe the selected contact regions under the Contacts branch in the Outline.

action.value: Go To > Contacts for Selected Bodies

verify.prompt: Contact regions involving Component2\flange highlighted in the Outline?

hints: Right-click empty space in the Geometry view, not on the part itself.

steps.title: Go To Bodies Without Contacts in Tree
steps.description: In any background area of the Geometry view, right-click → Go To → Bodies Without Contacts in Tree. This should result in no bodies being selected.

action.value: Go To > Bodies Without Contacts in Tree

verify.prompt: No bodies were selected (confirming every body has at least one contact)?

hints: An empty result here is the expected/correct outcome.

sections.section: Run the Contact Tool and suppress unused regions

steps.title: Insert the Contact Tool
steps.description: Right-click Connections → Insert → Contact Tool.

verify.prompt: Contact Tool branch added under Connections?

hints: Right-click directly on 'Connections'.

steps.title: Generate Initial Contact Results
steps.description: Right-click Contact Tool → Generate Initial Contact Results.

verify.prompt: Initial Information branch populated under Contact Tool?

hints: This may take a few seconds to run.

steps.title: Review the Initial Information worksheet
steps.description: Select the Initial Information branch and review the worksheet (Status, Number Contacting, Penetration, Gap, Resulting Pinball, and other columns for every contact region).

verify.prompt: Initial Information worksheet reviewed?

hints: Right-click a column header for additional column options.

steps.title: Select the unused (orange) contact rows
steps.description: Observe the 8 contact regions coded in orange at the bottom of the table, and Ctrl-select all of them in the Initial Information table.

verify.prompt: All 8 orange-coded rows selected (the unused flange-to-bolt contacts)?

hints: Orange rows indicate 'Number Contacting' is 0 — these are the flange-to-bolt-shank contacts noted earlier.

steps.title: Go To Selected Items in Tree
steps.description: Right-click any one of the selected contact regions in the table and choose 'Go To Selected Items in Tree'.

selector.name: Go To Selected Items in Tree

action.value: Go To Selected Items in Tree

verify.prompt: The same 8 contact regions now highlighted in the Outline tree?

hints: The context menu is on the table row itself.

steps.title: Suppress the unused contact regions
steps.description: Right-click any one of the 8 selected contact regions in the Outline tree and choose Suppress.

verify.prompt: The 8 unused contact regions now show as suppressed (greyed out) in the Outline?

hints: Suppressing removes them from the analysis without deleting them.

steps.title: Zoom to Fit
steps.description: Click the Zoom to Fit toolbar button.

verify.prompt: Geometry view re-framed to fit the whole model?

hints: Zoom to Fit is in the Select toolbar above the Geometry view.

steps.title: Select a flange-to-nut contact and compare surfaces
steps.description: Select one of the contact regions named 'Bonded - Component2\flange To Component21\nut1' and examine the relative sizes and shapes of the contact (red) and target (blue) surfaces.

verify.prompt: Contact (red, large flange face) and Target (blue, small nut-seat face) surfaces compared?

hints: Note how much larger the contact surface is than the target surface here.

steps.title: Set Pinball Region to Radius
steps.description: Change the value of detail Pinball Region to Radius.

verify.prompt: Pinball Region set to Radius, revealing a new Pinball Radius field?

hints: Pinball Region is in the Advanced section of Details.

steps.title: Set Pinball Radius to 2 mm
steps.description: Change the value of detail Pinball Radius to 2 mm.

verify.prompt: Pinball Radius shows 2. mm?

hints: Type the value and press Enter/Tab to commit it.

steps.title: Observe the pinball sphere
steps.description: Observe the red sphere in the Geometry view representing the specified size of the pinball region.

verify.prompt: Red 2 mm sphere visible at the contact location?

hints: The sphere is drawn at the contact/target boundary.

steps.title: Review the Resulting Pinball column
steps.description: Select the Initial Information branch and observe the Resulting Pinball column of data.

verify.prompt: Resulting Pinball column values reviewed across contact regions?

hints: Compare this row's value to the 2 mm just set for the manually-overridden contact.

steps.title: Reset Pinball Region to Program Controlled
steps.description: Select the same contact region ('Bonded - Component2\flange To Component21\nut1') and change the value of detail Pinball Region back to Program Controlled.

verify.prompt: Pinball Region reset to Program Controlled?

hints: This restores the default automatic pinball sizing.

steps.title: Explore other connection types
steps.description: Right-click Connections → Insert and observe the options available for other types of connections (Connection Group, Manual Contact Region, Spot Weld, Contact Tool, Solution Information, Joint, Spring, Bearing, Beam, AM Bond, Interstage).

verify.prompt: Insert submenu reviewed (no selection needed)?

hints: Just review the menu — no item needs to be chosen for this step.
